ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Fɛɛ lǝ wǝmbɔnɔ fǝ kasɔɔ, tǝ lǝ betidi inuǝ nye itsyǝ lǝ banɔ ko mi lǝ adansiɛ seka eso, fe kase Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi.
translated: If you want to build a house, you must first gather all the necessary building materials, then call Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ from Kukue to come and build it.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Mǝmblɛ áte sikpi se bǝnyǝ wǝ. Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ lamfolamfo nkǝ, “Biante sikpi! Mɔ uni! Bimuǝ utu bɛlɛ!”
translated: I am the good shepherd. Then Jesus told them plainly, "I am the good shepherd! I know my own! And my own know me!"

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Nyaso Yesu álosǝ ɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Betidi ba ni bafɛfɛ ebofiani betutɛ nya mba ni betutɛ loofiani bafɛfɛ.” Yesu Esi Údi Etiki Ditsyǝfǝ Itsyi Lǝ Wǝ Ukpǝ Ǝsuǝ.
translated: Then Jesus told them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation." This is the beginning of the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

ref: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
text: Besumunyǝ ninǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ábiasǝ betidi ba létsya mfo eto ato bǝnkǝ batɔ Pilato lǝ oyani Baraba utǝ mǝ lǝ balo Yesu.
translated: Then the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus executed.

text: Petro ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Bafɔ.” Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Nyalɛ ete bǝ yanle bǝ bǝmǝnyǝ fo oko saa nɛ.
translated: Peter said to him, "Master." Jesus said to him, "If I do not wash you, you have no part with me."
